title: Chief Minister Lalduhoma graces MISMA Fight Night
content:
128/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 30th May, 2026:Mizoram Mixed Martial Arts Association (MISMA) organized MISMA Fight Night was held on Saturday at R. Dengthuama Indoor Stadium, Mualpui. The event was graced by Shri Lalduhoma, Chief Minister as the Chief Guest, while UFC Flyweight Champion Joshua Van attended the occasion as Special Guest.

The Chief Minister, in his address, stated that honoring Joshua “The Fearless” Van, the first-ever UFC Champion from Southeast Asia, was a matter of great pride for the people of Mizoram. He emphasized that the Mizos, Chins, and Kukis belong to one ethnic family and that maintaining unity and brotherhood among them is of utmost importance. Joshua Van, he said, stands before the world as a representative of his people, and his achievements further strengthen the bonds and solidarity among these communities.

The Chief Minister praised Joshua Van’s remarkable accomplishments, highlighting his professional record of 17 victories, including 9 knockouts. He remarked that such achievements go beyond statistics and serve as an inspiration and challenge to young people. Referring to his own attainment of an Honorary 7th Dan Black Belt from the World Pro Taekwondo Headquarters Federation, Shri Lalduhoma stressed that reaching such heights requires self-discipline, perseverance, and strong mental fortitude. The Chief Minister also expressed his delight that he had received an Honorary Black Belt from Shariq Bapu, Senior Vice President of the Asian MMA Federation.

Shri Lalduhoma further noted that Joshua Van’s success encourages Mizo youth to set higher goals and confidently face the challenges of the world. Expressing his gratitude to the UFC champion, he assured him that Mizoram would continue to support him in all his future endeavors.

Shri Lalnghinglova Hmar, Minister of Sports and Youth Services, in his address informed the gathering that approval had been secured under the North East Special Infrastructure Development Scheme – Other Than Roads Infrastructure (NESIDS-OTRI) of the Ministry of DoNER for the construction of a Centre of Excellence for Boxing and various contact sports in Mizoram, at a cost of ₹38 crore. He described the approval as a significant achievement for the state’s sports sector.

The results of the MISMA Fight Night bouts are as follows:

Featherweight (65.8 kg Amateur Bout): Lalramsangliana (SPES Gym, MISMA) defeated Jeff Diengdoh (HR MMA Gym, Meghalaya). The trophy was presented by Shri Prasad Gaitonde, General Secretary, MMA India.

Super Lightweight (-74.8 kg Semi-Pro Bout): David Lalmalsawma (SPES Gym, MISMA) defeated Zacharia (Elite Gym, MISMA). The trophy was presented by Shri Shariq Bapu, Senior Vice President, Asian MMA Federation.

Co-Main Event Featherweight (-65.8 kg Semi-Pro Bout): Lalnunmawia (SPES Gym, MISMA) defeated Lalruatmawia (Zonet Mega Boxing Champion). The championship belt was presented by Joshua Van and Shri L.V. Lalthantluanga, Director of Zonet.

Main Event Bantamweight (-61.2 kg Professional Bout): Lalnunsanga (SPES Gym, MISMA) defeated Thangsanglian (Genesis Gym, Manipur) by technical knockout (TKO). The bantamweight championship belt was presented by Chief Minister Shri Lalduhoma and Sports Minister Shri Lalnghinglova Hmar.

Joshua Van also addressed the gathering, and during the event, several local community organizations, including CRCM, Chin Community, Zofa Global, Zoro, the Muay Thai Association of Mizoram, and Assam MMA, presented tokens of appreciation and honors to the UFC Champion.

The Chief Minister was accompanied by Shri B. Lalchhanzova, Minister of IPRP&S, Prof. Lalnilawma, Minister of RD&AD, Shri Lalfamkima, Deputy Speaker, Shri Lalmuanpuia Punte, MLA and Adviser to the Chief Minister, Lt. Col. Clement Lalhmingthanga, MLA (Honorary President, MISMA), and Smt. Baryl Vanneihsangi, MLA. A large number of leaders from the Central YMA, sports leaders, government officials, and representatives from the International MMA Federation were also present in the event.

Attachment

title: Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma’n MISMA Fight Night hmanpui
content:
777/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 30th May, 2026:Inrinni zan khan Mizoram Mixed Martial Arts Association (MISMA) buaitsaih MISMA Fight Night chu R. Dengṭhuama Indoor Stadium, Mualpui-ah neih a ni a. He hun hi Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma chuan khuallian niin a hmanpui. Special Guest UFC Flyweight Champion Joshua Van chuan a telpui bawk a ni.

Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma chuan thu sawiin, Southeast Asia atanga UFC Champion hmasa ber Joshua 'The Fearless' Van chawimawi a ni chu Zoram mipuite tan chhinchhiah tlak tak a nih thu a sawi a. Chief Minister chuan Mizote, Chin leh Kuki-te hi hnam pakhat leh chhungkua pakhat niin, inzawmna leh unauna tha neih a pawimawhzia a sawi bawk. Joshua Van chu a mite aiawhin khawvel hmaah a ding a, chu chuan hnam inpumkhatna leh inpawhna a tichak zual a ni, a ti.

Pu Lalduhoma chuan Joshua Van chu chhuanawm a tih zia sawiin, a professional record ropui tak - vawi 17 hnehna chang tawhin, vawi 9 knockout (17 wins with 9 knockouts) hmanga hnehna a lo neih tawh chu a sawi a, he record hian score mai piah lamah ṭhalaite tan chona a siam a ni, a ti. World Pro Taekwondo Headquarters Federation atanga 7th Dan Black Belt lo dawng tawh a nih angin Chief Minister chuan dinhmun sang tak thlen nan chuan mahni inthunun theihna te, teirei peihna leh rilru chhel tak (strong mental fortitude) neih a ṭulzia a sawi a; he hunah hian International MMA Federation aiawhin Shariq Bapu (Senior VP, Asian MMA Federation) hnen atangin Honorary Black Belt chawimawina sâng tak a dawng chu lawmawm a tih thu a sawi bawk.

Chief Minister chuan Joshua Van-a hlawhtlinna hian Mizo thalaite chu tum sang zawk neih theihna leh, huai taka khawvel hmachhawn ngam zel turin a fuih a ni, a ti. Joshua Van hnenah lawmthu sawiin, Mizoram chuan a hmachhawp zawng zawngah a tawiawm zel dawn a ni tih a sawi bawk.

Sports Minister Pu Lalnghinglova Hmar chuan kalkhawmte hnenah MISMA Fight khuhhawnnan thusawiin, Mizorama Boxing leh contact sports hrang hrang 'Centre of Excellence' sakna tur cheng vaibelchhe 38 chu Ministry of DoNER hnuaia 'North East Special Infrastructure Development Scheme - Other Than Roads Infrastructure (NESIDS-OTRI)' atangin approval an hmuh tawh thu chu sawiin, lawmawm a tih thu a sawi a ni.

MISMA Fight Night inelna (bouts) kal dan tlangpui chu hetiang hi a ni:

Featherweight (65.8 kgs Amateur Bout): Lalramsangliana (SPES Gym, MISMA) leh Jeff Diengdoh (HR MMA Gym, Meghalaya) te intumin, Lalramsangliana chuan hnehna a chang a. Trophy hi Pu Prasad Gaitonde, GS, MMA India in hlanna hun a hmang a ni.

Super Lightweight (-74.8 kgs Semi-Pro Bout): David Lalmalsawma (SPES Gym, MISMA) leh Zacharia (Elite Gym, MISMA) te intumin, David Lalmalsawma chu a chak a, trophy hi Pu Sharif Bapu, Sr. VP, Asian MMA Fed. chuan a hlan a ni.

Co- Main Event Featherweight (-65.8 kgs Semi-Pro bout): Lalnunmawia (SPES Gym, MISMA) leh Lalruatmawia (Zonet Mega Boxing Champion) te intumin, Lalnunmawia chuan hnehna a chang a, Belt hi Joshua Van leh Pu LV Lalthantluanga, Zonet Director ten an hlan a ni.

Main Event Bantamweight (-61.2 kgs Professional Bout): Lalnunsanga (SPES Gym, MISMA) leh Thangsanglian (Genesis Gym, Manipur) te intumin, Lalnunsanga chuan technical knockout in hnehna a chang a. He bantamweight belt hi Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma leh Sports Minister Pu Lalnghinglova Hmar ten an hlan a ni.

He hunah hian Joshua Van pawhin thu a sawi a, tualchhung khawtlang community hrang hrang: CRCM, Chin Community, Zofa Global, Zoro, Muay Thai Association of Mizoram leh Assam MMA te chuan UFC Champion Joshua Van hnenah chawimawina an hlan bawk.

He Fight Night-ah hian IPRP&S Minister Pu B. Lalchhanzova, RD&AD Minister Prof. Lalnilawma, Deputy Speaker Pu Lalfamkima, Pu Lalmuanpuia Punte, MLA (Adviser to CM), Lt. Col. Clement Lalhmingthanga, MLA (Hony. President, MISMA) leh Pi Baryl Vanneihsangi, MLA  te’n Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma an ṭawiawm a. Central YMA hruaitute, Sports lama hruaitute, sawrkar officials te leh International MMA Federation aṭangin ṭhahnem tak an tel bawk.

Attachment
